title: Sewing Machine Operators
description: Operate or tend sewing machines to join, reinforce, decorate, or perform related sewing operations in the manufacture of garment or nongarment products.

tasks.text: Monitor machine operation to detect problems such as defective stitching, breaks in thread, or machine malfunctions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Place spools of thread, cord, or other materials on spindles, insert bobbins, and thread ends through machine guides and components.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Position items under needles, using marks on machines, clamps, templates, or cloth as guides.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Guide garments or garment parts under machine needles and presser feet to sew parts together.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Remove holding devices and finished items from machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Match cloth pieces in correct sequences prior to sewing them, and verify that dye lots and patterns match.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fold or stretch edges or lengths of items while sewing to facilitate forming specified sections.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut excess material or thread from finished products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select supplies such as fasteners and thread, according to job requirements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine and measure finished articles to verify conformance to standards, using rulers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Start and operate or tend machines, such as single or double needle serging and flat-bed felling machines, to automatically join, reinforce, or decorate material or articles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record quantities of materials processed.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Turn knobs, screws, and dials to adjust settings of machines, according to garment styles and equipment performance.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Attach tape, trim, appliques, or elastic to specified garments or garment parts, according to item specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Repair or alter items by adding replacement parts or missing stitches.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform equipment maintenance tasks such as replacing needles, sanding rough areas of needles, or cleaning and oiling sewing machines.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Mount attachments, such as needles, cutting blades, or pattern plates, and adjust machine guides according to specifications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Cut materials according to specifications, using blades, scissors, or electric knives.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect garments, and examine repair tags and markings on garments to locate defects or damage, and mark errors as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Attach buttons, hooks, zippers, fasteners, or other accessories to fabric, using feeding hoppers or clamp holders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Position material or articles in clamps, templates, or hoop frames prior to automatic operation of machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Draw markings or pin appliques on fabric to obtain variations in design.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Tape or twist together thread or cord to repair breaks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Baste edges of material to align and temporarily secure parts for final assembly.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Position and mark patterns on materials to prepare for sewing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform specialized or automatic sewing machine functions, such as buttonhole making or tacking.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
